title: Selling Stock Photos Without PayPal: An Honest Side-by-Side Breakdown — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan trying to sell stock photos, you're likely facing a frustrating reality: PayPal is not available to you. This restrictions creates a huge barrier for creators like you, who want to monetize your work without jumping through hoops. In this post, we'll take a hard look at what each option offers, how they handle restricted countries, payment speed and fees, automation, and ultimately, which one to choose.
What Each Option Actually Offers

PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are among the most popular platforms for selling digital products, but they have strict guidelines that exclude certain countries. PayPal, for example, isn't available in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan due to banking restrictions. These platforms require creators to set up an account, which can lead to lengthy verification processes, and often come with hefty fees. In contrast, the Full Stack Bundle provides a complete store infrastructure that allows creators to sell without setting up a PayPal or Stripe account, making it a more convenient option for those in restricted countries.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

When it comes to handling restricted countries, the popular platforms fall short. PayPal, for instance, uses a complex system that determines which countries are eligible for services, leaving creators in restricted countries with few alternatives. Meanwhile, the Full Stack Bundle acknowledges the limitations of these platforms and provides a more inclusive solution, allowing creators to sell digital products without worrying about PayPal or Stripe's restrictions.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

When it comes to payment speed and fees, platforms like PayPal and Stripe can be costly. PayPal charges a 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction fee, while Stripe charges 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction fee for US-based payments. The Full Stack Bundle, on the other hand, offers a one-time payment option, eliminating subscription fees and providing a more transparent pricing structure.
The Full Stack Bundle is a more inclusive solution for digital creators in restricted countries.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Ultimately, when choosing a platform to sell digital products, consider your specific needs and circumstances. If you're in a restricted country like Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan, the Full Stack Bundle is a more suitable option due to its PayPal-free and Stripe-free infrastructure. However, if you're willing to jump through hoops and create additional accounts, the popular platforms might be a viable option for you.
How do I sell digital products if PayPal is not available in my country?

The direct answer is NOWPayments combined with the Core Shop Engine. NOWPayments is a crypto payment processor that requires no KYC from merchants and no account creation from buyers. It supports over 300 coins and sends a webhook to your backend when a payment confirms on-chain. The Core Shop Engine receives that webhook, verifies the transaction, generates a receipt, and delivers the product automatically. The key difference between this and a platform workaround is that it is not a workaround — it is a different infrastructure layer entirely. There is no platform that can suspend your access, no compliance team that can flag your account, and no payout schedule that delays your revenue. Your funds go directly to a wallet you control the moment a transaction confirms.
You don't need to set up a PayPal or Stripe account to use the Full Stack Bundle.
